$comment: Hidden Skill Taxonomy v2 — extracted verbatim from plans/prd/cognify-system-change-v2-2026-07.md §5.5 (D20, full adoption). Dimension keys use CODE names: PRD 'Pacing' = code 'delivery' (see plans/prd/terminology-map.md). Labels are Title Case for UI/prompt surfaces; definitions are the doc's 'What it means' column. src/types/sub-skills.ts is GENERATED from this file via scripts/taxonomy/generate-sub-skills.mjs — edit here, then regenerate.

skills.label: Audience Calibration
skills.definition: Matching vocabulary, complexity, and assumptions to the listener's knowledge level

skills.label: Jargon Translation
skills.definition: Replacing technical, tribal, academic, or corporate language with accessible language

skills.label: Vocabulary Precision
skills.definition: Choosing the exact word rather than a vague or overbroad one

skills.label: Concreteness
skills.definition: Using tangible examples, images, comparisons, or cases instead of abstractions

skills.label: Idea Isolation
skills.definition: Explaining one idea at a time rather than stacking multiple concepts together

skills.label: Definition Discipline
skills.definition: Defining key terms before using them heavily

skills.label: Abstraction Laddering
skills.definition: Moving smoothly from abstract principle to concrete example, or vice versa

skills.label: Analogy Selection
skills.definition: Choosing analogies that simplify rather than distort

skills.label: Example Grounding
skills.definition: Making abstract claims understandable through real-world examples

skills.label: Assumption Checking
skills.definition: Avoiding unexplained assumptions about what the listener already knows

skills.label: Ambiguity Reduction
skills.definition: Removing unclear referents, vague pronouns, fuzzy claims, and 'this/that/it' confusion

skills.label: Referential Clarity
skills.definition: Making clear what each noun, pronoun, or example refers to

skills.label: Sentence Simplicity
skills.definition: Using clean, speakable sentence structures rather than tangled clauses or run-ons

skills.label: Mental-Model Matching
skills.definition: Explaining from the listener's current model toward the new model

skills.label: Common-Ground Creation
skills.definition: Starting from something the listener already understands

skills.label: Signal-to-Noise Control
skills.definition: Removing distracting side points, verbal clutter, and unnecessary caveats

skills.label: Listener-First Sequencing
skills.definition: Presenting information in the order the listener needs, not the order the speaker thought of it

skills.label: Distinction-Making
skills.definition: Clearly separating similar concepts that are easy to confuse

skills.label: Lexical Specificity
skills.definition: Replacing 'stuff,' 'things,' 'kind of,' and 'a lot' with sharper language

skills.label: Cognitive Load Reduction
skills.definition: Making the message easy to process by chunking, simplifying, and sequencing

skills.label: Question Fidelity
skills.definition: Answering the actual question asked rather than a nearby question

skills.label: Plain-English Conversion
skills.definition: Translating expert knowledge into ordinary speech

skills.label: Takeaway Clarity
skills.definition: Making the one thing the listener should remember obvious

skills.label: Bottom-Line Discipline
skills.definition: Giving the main answer or recommendation early

skills.label: Opening Hook
skills.definition: Beginning with a sentence that orients attention and creates relevance

skills.label: Signposting
skills.definition: Using explicit verbal markers: 'There are three reasons,' 'First,' 'The tradeoff is...'

skills.label: Argument Hierarchy
skills.definition: Separating main points from supporting details

skills.label: Structural Fit
skills.definition: Choosing the right structure for the situation: STAR, BLUF, PREP, Pyramid, AIDA, problem-solution

skills.label: Narrative Arc
skills.definition: Creating a clear beginning, tension/change, and resolution

skills.label: STAR Fluency
skills.definition: Balancing Situation, Task, Action, Result without drowning in background

skills.label: BLUF Fluency
skills.definition: Leading with the conclusion before detail

skills.label: PREP Fluency
skills.definition: Making a point, giving a reason, giving an example, and restating the point

skills.label: Pyramid Thinking
skills.definition: Starting with synthesis, then grouping supporting arguments

skills.label: MECE Grouping
skills.definition: Organizing points so they do not overlap excessively or leave major gaps

skills.label: Chunking
skills.definition: Grouping information into digestible units

skills.label: Transition Control
skills.definition: Moving from one idea to the next without abrupt jumps

skills.label: Coherence
skills.definition: Making every part feel connected to the main point

skills.label: Context-Ratio Control
skills.definition: Giving enough setup, but not so much that the point gets buried

skills.label: Sequence Logic
skills.definition: Ordering information chronologically, causally, by importance, or by decision relevance

skills.label: Causal Chain Construction
skills.definition: Showing how A leads to B leads to C

skills.label: Contrast Structure
skills.definition: Organizing around tradeoffs: option A vs. option B

skills.label: Problem-Solution Framing
skills.definition: Naming the problem, explaining why it matters, then offering a solution

skills.label: Decision Framing
skills.definition: Organizing speech around criteria, options, recommendation, and risk

skills.label: Recap and Closure
skills.definition: Ending with a clear takeaway rather than trailing off

skills.label: Thread Maintenance
skills.definition: Keeping the main thread alive while adding examples or details

skills.label: Listener Navigation
skills.definition: Helping the listener always know 'where we are' in the response

skills.label: Response Scoping
skills.definition: Matching the answer size to the question, audience, and time available

skills.label: Relevance Filtering
skills.definition: Including only details that serve the listener's need

skills.label: Information Density
skills.definition: Saying more meaning per word

skills.label: Repetition Control
skills.definition: Avoiding saying the same idea multiple ways without added value

skills.label: Filler Reduction
skills.definition: Reducing 'um,' 'uh,' 'like,' 'you know,' and other low-value verbal padding

skills.label: Hedging Control
skills.definition: Reducing unnecessary 'kind of,' 'sort of,' 'maybe,' and 'I guess'

skills.label: Real-Time Editing
skills.definition: Cutting a point while speaking when it becomes unnecessary

skills.label: End Discipline
skills.definition: Stopping after the answer is complete

skills.label: Time-Box Awareness
skills.definition: Speaking within 30, 60, 90, or 120-second constraints

skills.label: Compression
skills.definition: Condensing a complex idea into a shorter form without losing the core meaning

skills.label: Priority Selection
skills.definition: Choosing the strongest point instead of listing every possible point

skills.label: Context Minimization
skills.definition: Giving only the setup required for the listener to understand

skills.label: Example Economy
skills.definition: Using one strong example rather than several weak examples

skills.label: Caveat Discipline
skills.definition: Including caveats only when they materially change the claim

skills.label: Qualifier Discipline
skills.definition: Avoiding unnecessary softeners that reduce force without increasing accuracy

skills.label: Verbal Clutter Removal
skills.definition: Removing throat-clearing phrases like 'I think what I would say is...'

skills.label: One-Point Discipline
skills.definition: Making one point well instead of three points poorly

skills.label: Answer-First Compression
skills.definition: Starting with the answer, then using detail only if needed

skills.label: Low-Value Detail Deletion
skills.definition: Cutting names, dates, backstory, or process details that do not matter

skills.label: Redundancy Detection
skills.definition: Recognizing when a sentence repeats rather than advances the thought

skills.label: Strong Close
skills.definition: Ending with a clean final sentence instead of drifting

skills.label: Conversational Minimalism
skills.definition: Being brief without sounding cold, evasive, or abrupt

skills.label: Claim Clarity
skills.definition: Making the central claim explicit

skills.label: Claim Support
skills.definition: Supporting claims with reasons, evidence, examples, or mechanisms

skills.label: Reasoning Chain Logic
skills.definition: Showing how one idea leads logically to another

skills.label: First-Principles Reasoning
skills.definition: Grounding an answer in the most basic causes, incentives, constraints, or mechanisms

skills.label: Evidence Relevance
skills.definition: Using evidence that actually supports the claim

skills.label: Example Validity
skills.definition: Choosing examples that illustrate rather than distract

skills.label: Causal Reasoning
skills.definition: Distinguishing correlation, cause, mechanism, and consequence

skills.label: Tradeoff Awareness
skills.definition: Naming what is gained and what is sacrificed

skills.label: Counterargument Awareness
skills.definition: Recognizing the strongest objection to one's view

skills.label: Steel-Manning
skills.definition: Representing the opposing view fairly before responding

skills.label: Intellectual Honesty
skills.definition: Avoiding overclaiming, false certainty, or misleading simplification

skills.label: Epistemic Humility
skills.definition: Calibrating certainty to what is actually known

skills.label: Assumption Transparency
skills.definition: Naming the assumptions behind the answer

skills.label: Decision Criteria
skills.definition: Explaining what standard is being used to judge options

skills.label: Depth of Analysis
skills.definition: Moving beyond surface observation into why it matters

skills.label: Implication Drawing
skills.definition: Explaining what follows if the claim is true

skills.label: Relevance Discipline
skills.definition: Staying responsive to the actual prompt

skills.label: Precision of Conclusion
skills.definition: Ending with a nuanced, accurate version of the claim

skills.label: Problem Diagnosis
skills.definition: Identifying the real issue beneath the obvious issue

skills.label: Root-Cause Analysis
skills.definition: Distinguishing symptoms from underlying causes

skills.label: Synthesis
skills.definition: Combining multiple facts or perspectives into a higher-level point

skills.label: Perspective-Taking
skills.definition: Considering how different stakeholders would view the issue

skills.label: Probabilistic Thinking
skills.definition: Using likelihood, risk, and uncertainty rather than binary certainty

skills.label: Constraint Recognition
skills.definition: Noticing practical, ethical, financial, interpersonal, or time constraints

skills.label: Mechanism Explanation
skills.definition: Explaining how something works, not just that it works

skills.label: Scope Control
skills.definition: Knowing when a claim applies and when it does not

skills.label: Truthfulness Under Pressure
skills.definition: Maintaining accuracy when trying to sound confident

skills.label: Avoidance of Vacuity
skills.definition: Avoiding impressive-sounding but empty language

skills.label: Rate Awareness
skills.definition: Knowing whether one is speaking too fast, too slow, or appropriately

skills.label: Words-per-Minute Control
skills.definition: Adjusting speed to the context and listener

skills.label: Strategic Pausing
skills.definition: Whether or not pausing is used before or after important points

skills.label: Pause Placement
skills.definition: Pausing at idea boundaries rather than randomly

skills.label: Thought-Group Chunking
skills.definition: Speaking in digestible phrases rather than long streams

skills.label: Rhythm Variation
skills.definition: Avoiding monotone cadence by varying speed and intensity

skills.label: Emphasis Timing
skills.definition: Slowing or stressing the most important phrase

skills.label: Pressure Pacing
skills.definition: Staying controlled when nervous, challenged, or time-limited

skills.label: Silence Tolerance
skills.definition: Resisting the urge to fill every pause

skills.label: Filler-to-Pause Substitution
skills.definition: Replacing 'um' and 'like' with clean silence

skills.label: Acceleration Control
skills.definition: Avoiding speeding up during complex, emotional, or high-stakes moments

skills.label: Deceleration for Complexity
skills.definition: Slowing down when the idea is difficult

skills.label: Transition Pacing
skills.definition: Briefly pausing before shifting topics

skills.label: Processing-Space Creation
skills.definition: Giving the listener time to absorb key information

skills.label: Time Management
skills.definition: Ending within the assigned time without rushing the final point

skills.label: Recovery Pacing
skills.definition: Regaining rhythm after a stumble or correction

skills.label: Cadence Control
skills.definition: Developing a controlled, listenable speaking pattern

skills.label: Prosodic Alignment
skills.definition: Matching pitch, rhythm, and stress to meaning

skills.label: Emotional Tempo
skills.definition: Slowing down during serious moments and energizing during inspiring ones

skills.label: Conversational Turn-Holding
skills.definition: Using pauses and vocal cues to signal whether one is continuing or finished

skills.label: Monotony Avoidance
skills.definition: Preventing flat, same-speed delivery

skills.label: Listener-Sensitive Pacing
skills.definition: Adjusting speed for novices, non-native speakers, emotional listeners, or technical material

skills.label: Warmth
skills.definition: Sounding humane, approachable, and relationally aware

skills.label: Authority
skills.definition: Sounding credible, decisive, and appropriately firm

skills.label: Confidence
skills.definition: Speaking with grounded assurance rather than bravado

skills.label: Emotional Authenticity
skills.definition: Sounding sincere rather than performed or scripted

skills.label: Emotional Control
skills.definition: Staying composed under stress, criticism, conflict, or embarrassment

skills.label: Empathy
skills.definition: Acknowledging the listener's emotional reality

skills.label: Reassurance
skills.definition: Making the listener feel steadier without minimizing their concern

skills.label: Directness
skills.definition: Saying the necessary thing plainly

skills.label: Tact
skills.definition: Being honest without being needlessly harsh

skills.label: Diplomacy
skills.definition: Preserving relationships while addressing tension

skills.label: Accountability
skills.definition: Taking responsibility without self-erasure or defensiveness

skills.label: Nondefensiveness
skills.definition: Responding to critique without sounding attacked

skills.label: Assertiveness
skills.definition: Advocating clearly without aggression

skills.label: Humility
skills.definition: Lowering ego without lowering credibility

skills.label: Gravitas
skills.definition: Matching seriousness with controlled presence

skills.label: Enthusiasm
skills.definition: Showing energy and genuine interest without sounding manic

skills.label: Calmness
skills.definition: Reducing emotional volatility in the room

skills.label: Urgency
skills.definition: Communicating importance without panic

skills.label: Respect
skills.definition: Treating the listener as competent and worthy of consideration

skills.label: Formality Calibration
skills.definition: Matching register to setting: casual, professional, ceremonial, clinical, executive

skills.label: Power-Distance Calibration
skills.definition: Adjusting tone for senior leaders, peers, subordinates, clients, patients, or friends

skills.label: Cultural Sensitivity
skills.definition: Avoiding tone choices that may read differently across cultures

skills.label: Warmth-Authority Balance
skills.definition: Being kind without being weak, and firm without being cold

skills.label: Boundary-Setting
skills.definition: Saying no or naming limits clearly and respectfully

skills.label: Conflict De-escalation
skills.definition: Lowering heat while preserving substance

skills.label: Encouragement
skills.definition: Motivating without patronizing

skills.label: Curiosity
skills.definition: Sounding genuinely open rather than performatively neutral

skills.label: Credibility Through Restraint
skills.definition: Avoiding exaggeration, overselling, or emotional overacting

skills.label: Congruence
skills.definition: Making words, voice, and emotional stance align

skills.label: Listener Safety Creation
skills.definition: Making it easier for the listener to receive difficult information
